en: Got in at noon, left at midnight. That's a long day.
ko: 정오에 들어와서 자정에 나갔어요. 긴 하루였네요.

en: Good for you. Nothing wrong with a hard day's work.
ko: 힘든 하루 일하는 건 나쁘지 않아요.

en: So you say a man came in, and he reached across the counter
and, quote, he grabbed up the money from my register
ko: 내 금고에서 돈을 집어들고 도망쳤어요. 맞아요.
내 금고에서 돈을 집어들고 도망쳤어요. 맞아요.

en: And there was no one else in the store? Not at that time, no.
And since the camera system wasn't working at the time, you're the only one who saw the perpetrator.
Yeah. So this person came in, and they bought something.
ko: 그래서 이 사람이 들어와서 뭔가를 샀어요.

en: I think it was an Almond Joy. They bought an Almond Joy.
And when you rang them up, that's when they snatched the cash from the register.
Sounds like it happened pretty fast. But you say you got a good look at him, correct?
Yes. You must drink stronger coffee than I do,
ko: 당신은 제가 마시는 것보다 더 강한 커피를 마시는군요.

en: because after 11 hours on the job, I can barely see straight.
And it was dark out. Oh, he's the only guy that came in that night
